subtopics.content: Order positive and negative integers, decimals and fractions; use the symbols =, ≠, <, >, ≤, ≥

Apply the four operations, including formal written methods, to integers, decimals and simple fractions (proper and improper), and mixed numbers – all both positive and negative; understand and use place value
Recognise and use relationships between operations, including inverse operations; use conventional notation for priority of operations, including brackets, powers, roots and reciprocals
Use concepts and vocabulary of prime numbers, factors, multiples, HCF, LCM, prime factorisation, including using product notation and the unique factorisation theorem; systematic listing strategies
Apply systematic listing strategies, including use of the product rule for counting (Higher)
Use positive integer powers and associated real roots; recognise powers of 2, 3, 4, 5; estimate powers and roots of any given positive number (Higher)
Calculate with roots, and with integer indices (Foundation); integer and fractional indices (Higher)
Calculate exactly with fractions and multiples of π (Foundation); calculate exactly with fractions, surds and multiples of π; simplify surd expressions and rationalise denominators (Higher)
Calculate with and interpret standard form A × 10ⁿ, where 1 ≤ A < 10 and n is an integer
Work interchangeably with terminating decimals and their corresponding fractions; change recurring decimals into their corresponding fractions and vice versa (Higher)
Identify and work with fractions in ratio problems
Interpret fractions and percentages as operators
Use standard units of mass, length, time, money and other measures using decimal quantities
Estimate answers; check calculations using approximation and estimation, including answers obtained using technology
Round numbers and measures to an appropriate degree of accuracy; use inequality notation to specify simple error intervals due to truncation or rounding
Apply and interpret limits of accuracy, including upper and lower bounds (Higher)
Use and interpret algebraic manipulation: ab, 3y, a², a³, a²b, a/b, coefficients as fractions, brackets
Substitute numerical values into formulae and expressions, including scientific formulae
Understand and use concepts and vocabulary of expressions, equations, formulae, identities, inequalities, terms and factors

Simplify and manipulate algebraic expressions by collecting like terms, multiplying over bracket, taking out common factors, expanding binomials, factorising quadratics, simplifying sums/products/powers including laws of indices; surds and algebraic fractions (Higher)
Understand and use standard mathematical formulae; rearrange formulae to change the subject
Know the difference between an equation and an identity; argue mathematically to show equivalence; use algebra to support and construct arguments and proofs (Higher)
Interpret simple expressions as functions with inputs and outputs; inverse and composite functions (Higher, formal notation expected)
Work with coordinates in all four quadrants
Plot straight-line graphs; use y=mx+c to identify parallel lines (Foundation); parallel and perpendicular lines (Higher); find equation through two points or one point with given gradient
Identify and interpret gradients and intercepts of linear functions graphically and algebraically
Identify and interpret roots, intercepts, turning points of quadratic functions graphically; deduce roots algebraically; turning points by completing the square (Higher)
Recognise, sketch and interpret graphs of linear, quadratic, simple cubic, reciprocal y=1/x; exponential y=kˣ and trig functions sin x, cos x, tan x (Higher)
Sketch translations and reflections of a given function (Higher)
Plot and interpret graphs in real contexts to find approximate solutions to problems including kinematic problems
Calculate or estimate gradients and areas under graphs; interpret results in distance-time, velocity-time and financial contexts (Higher, no calculus)
Recognise and use equation of circle with centre at origin; find equation of tangent at a given point (Higher)
Solve linear equations in one unknown algebraically; find approximate solutions using a graph
Solve quadratic equations by factorising (Foundation); by factorising, completing the square and quadratic formula (Higher); approximate solutions using a graph
Solve two simultaneous equations in two variables (linear/linear Foundation; linear/linear or linear/quadratic Higher); approximate solutions using a graph
Find approximate solutions to equations numerically using iteration (Higher)

subtopics.subtopicName: Form and solve equations from contexts
subtopics.content: Translate situations into algebraic expressions or formulae; derive equation(s), solve and interpret the solution
Solve linear inequalities in one variable; represent on number line (Foundation); linear in one or two variables and quadratic in one variable, set notation and graphs (Higher)
Generate terms of a sequence from a term-to-term or position-to-term rule
Recognise and use sequences: triangular, square, cube, arithmetic progressions, Fibonacci, quadratic, geometric progressions rⁿ where n is integer and r is rational > 0 (or surd Higher)
Deduce expressions to calculate the nth term of linear sequences (Foundation); linear and quadratic sequences (Higher)

topics.topicName: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

subtopics.content: Change freely between related standard units and compound units in numerical and algebraic contexts
Use scale factors, scale diagrams and maps
Express one quantity as a fraction of another, where fraction is less than 1 or greater than 1
Use ratio notation, including reduction to simplest form
Divide a quantity in a given part:part or part:whole ratio; apply to real contexts
Express a multiplicative relationship between two quantities as a ratio or a fraction
Understand and use proportion as equality of ratios

subtopics.subtopicName: Ratios to fractions and linear functions
subtopics.content: Relate ratios to fractions and to linear functions

Define percentage; interpret percentages and changes as fraction or decimal; percentage of another; compare; percentage greater than 100%; percentage change including original value and simple interest
Solve problems involving direct and inverse proportion, including graphical and algebraic representations
Use compound units: speed, rates of pay, unit pricing, density and pressure
Compare using ratio notation; links to similarity and scale factors
Understand inverse proportion; construct and interpret equations for direct and inverse proportion (Higher)
Interpret gradient of straight line as rate of change; recognise and interpret graphs for direct and inverse proportion
Interpret gradient at point on curve as instantaneous rate of change; average and instantaneous rate of change (Higher, no calculus)
Set up, solve and interpret growth and decay problems including compound interest; general iterative processes (Higher)
Use conventional terms and notation: points, lines, vertices, edges, planes, parallel, perpendicular, right angles, polygons, regular polygons, symmetries; standard conventions for triangles
Use ruler and compass constructions: perpendicular bisector, perpendicular from point to line, bisecting an angle; construct figures and solve loci problems
Apply properties of angles at a point, on straight line, vertically opposite; alternate and corresponding angles on parallel lines; sum of angles in triangle and polygon
Derive and apply properties of special quadrilaterals (square, rectangle, parallelogram, trapezium, kite, rhombus) and triangles
Use basic congruence criteria for triangles (SSS, SAS, ASA, RHS)
Apply angle facts, congruence, similarity, quadrilateral properties to derive results including Pythagoras' theorem
Identify, describe and construct congruent and similar shapes: rotation, reflection, translation, enlargement including fractional (and negative Higher) scale factors
Describe changes and invariance achieved by combinations of rotations, reflections and translations (Higher)
Identify and apply circle definitions and properties: centre, radius, chord, diameter, circumference, tangent, arc, sector, segment
Apply and prove standard circle theorems concerning angles, radii, tangents and chords (Higher)
Solve geometrical problems on coordinate axes
Identify properties of faces, surfaces, edges and vertices of cubes, cuboids, prisms, cylinders, pyramids, cones, spheres
Construct and interpret plans and elevations of 3D shapes
Use standard units of measure and related concepts
Measure line segments and angles in geometric figures; interpret maps, scale drawings and bearings
Know and apply formulae: area of triangles, parallelograms, trapezia; volume of cuboids and right prisms
Circumference and area of circle; perimeters and areas including circles; surface area and volume of spheres, pyramids, cones and composite solids
Calculate arc lengths, angles and areas of sectors of circles
Apply concepts of congruence and similarity; relationships between lengths, areas and volumes in similar figures (Higher)
Pythagoras' theorem; trig ratios sin, cos, tan for right-angled triangles in 2D (Foundation); general triangles in 2D and 3D (Higher)
Know exact values of sin θ and cos θ for θ = 0°, 30°, 45°, 60°, 90°; tan θ for θ = 0°, 30°, 45°, 60°
Know and apply sine rule and cosine rule to find unknown lengths and angles (Higher)
Know and apply Area = ½ab sin C to calculate area, sides or angles of any triangle (Higher)
Describe translations as 2D vectors
Apply addition and subtraction of vectors, multiplication by scalar, diagrammatic and column representations; use vectors for geometric arguments and proofs (Higher)
Record, describe and analyse frequency of outcomes using tables and frequency trees
Apply randomness, fairness, equally likely events to calculate expected outcomes
Relate relative expected frequencies to theoretical probability; 0-1 probability scale
Apply property that probabilities of exhaustive set of outcomes sum to one; mutually exclusive events sum to one
Understand empirical unbiased samples tend towards theoretical probability with increasing sample size
Enumerate sets systematically using tables, grids, Venn diagrams and tree diagrams
Construct theoretical possibility spaces for single and combined experiments; calculate theoretical probabilities
Calculate probability of independent and dependent combined events including tree diagrams
Calculate and interpret conditional probabilities through expected frequencies with two-way tables, tree diagrams and Venn diagrams (Higher)
Infer properties of populations from a sample; know limitations of sampling
Interpret and construct frequency tables, bar charts, pie charts, pictograms, vertical line charts, tables and line graphs for time series
Construct and interpret histograms with equal and unequal class intervals and cumulative frequency graphs (Higher)
Interpret, analyse and compare distributions: graphical representation; measures of central tendency (median, mean, mode) and spread (range, outliers, quartiles, IQR Higher); box plots (Higher)
Apply statistics to describe a population
Use and interpret scatter graphs of bivariate data; recognise correlation; draw lines of best fit; interpolate and extrapolate knowing dangers
